text.title: AI Capabilities in Design Systems
text.summary: AI amplification principle, required prerequisites, capability table, and tool landscape for AI-enhanced design systems.

rule.statement: AI will amplify the existing design system foundation — it will not create one from nothing. Do not use AI to build foundational design system structure.
rule.rationale: AI-generated component variants inherit semantic errors from the seed component. A broken foundation produces broken variants at scale. Human-led system design must precede AI automation.

list.item: Well-defined design tokens (color, spacing, typography, shadow)
Systematic component structure with clear naming conventions
Style Dictionary or equivalent as single source of truth
At least 10 components before AI documentation automation pays off
Component documentation: generate props table, usage examples, accessibility notes, do/don't guidance
Token audit: detect hardcoded values (hex colors, px spacing) that should be token references
Variant generation: scale out size, state, density variants from seed component
Inconsistency detection: find components not following naming or token conventions
Dark mode adaptation: suggest token mappings for light/dark theme pairs
Figma REST API — read token/component data programmatically
Figma Tokens plugin — export token JSON for Style Dictionary pipeline
Style Dictionary — build token transforms across platforms (CSS, iOS, Android)
Storybook — component documentation host; agent generates .stories.tsx and .mdx
Chromatic — visual regression testing for AI-generated component variants
Supernova — design token management with multi-platform export; REST API
Specify — design asset + token sync between Figma and code; REST API
token-transformer — convert Figma Tokens plugin output to Style Dictionary format
